description:
A small web-app prototype has been left in “/home/user/app”.  
  As part of a quick vulnerability sweep, you (the penetration tester) have to identify every **world-writable regular file** inside that directory tree and deliver a concise report.

  1. Search recursively beneath “/home/user/app” for every regular file that has the Unix “others-write” bit set (octal **0002**).  
  2. Send the absolute paths of all such files, one per line, to a new text file named  
     /home/user/world_writable_report.txt
  3. Do **not** include directories, symbolic links, blank lines, headings, or any extra commentary—only the absolute paths.  
  4. If there are **no** matches, the file must contain the single uppercase word  
     NONE  
     on its own line.
  5. Complete the whole task with a single shell command line (pipelines are fine; avoid running multiple separate commands).

  The automated checker will inspect “/home/user/world_writable_report.txt”.  
  It must match one (and only one) of the following exact byte-for-byte patterns:

  • “/home/user/app/db.sqlite\n”  
    (path followed by a single newline)

  • “NONE\n”

  Nothing else will be accepted.
truth:
Pre-existing filesystem layout and permissions:

  Directory  : /home/user/app                (mode 0755, owner user)
  Files      : /home/user/app/config.yml     (mode 0644, owner user)
             : /home/user/app/db.sqlite      (mode 0666, owner user)  ← world-writable
             : /home/user/app/script.sh      (mode 0755, owner user)

  The correct result when the task starts is therefore one match:
      /home/user/app/db.sqlite

  After a correct solution the file
      /home/user/world_writable_report.txt
  must exist, be world-readable (0644 is fine) and contain exactly:

      /home/user/app/db.sqlite\n

  Any deviation (extra whitespace, additional paths, missing newline) will fail the automated test.
name: task_000000_d7728998
